title: thymus albanus subvar. lumbardensis
intro: Thymus albanus subvar. lumbardensis, commonly known as the Lumbardian thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant native to the mountainous regions of Albania. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic qualities, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Lumbardian thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and flu. Traditionally, Lumbardian thyme is prepared through herbal infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
